note: Texas has NOT expanded Medicaid. Adults without children generally do not qualify for Medicaid in Texas unless disabled or elderly.

programs.name: SNAP (Texas)
programs.description: Texas administers federal SNAP with standard federal eligibility rules. Apply online at YourTexasBenefits.com.

application.where: YourTexasBenefits.com or local HHSC benefits office

programs.name: Texas Medicaid
programs.description: Texas Medicaid is very restricted for adults. It covers: pregnant women (up to 198% FPL), children (up to 200% FPL), and adults with disabilities/elderly. Working adults without children generally do NOT qualify.

income.note: Texas has NOT expanded Medicaid. Very few working-age adults without disabilities qualify.

required_documents: Proof of pregnancy or disability if applicable

programs.name: Texas Works (TANF)
programs.full_name: Texas Works — Temporary Assistance for Needy Families
programs.description: Texas's TANF program provides modest cash assistance to families with children. Benefits are among the lowest in the nation.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Family of 3 receives approximately $256/month — one of the lowest TANF amounts in the US.

income.note: Very low income limits — generally below 12% FPL for initial eligibility
